claim_text: Although LPIPS and other DFDs28–33 outperform commonly used IQ metrics in measuring the perceptual quality of natural images, the relationship between the perceptual quality of MR images and DFDs is unknown.

sub_claims.text: The gu2022 challenge results show that deep-feature-distance (DFD) methods (such as LPIPS, DISTS, PieAPP, SWD, and the deep-learning-based participating teams) outperform commonly used / traditional IQ metrics (PSNR, SSIM, FSIM, NIQE, PI) in measuring the perceptual quality of natural images.

evidence.snippet: Almost all of them have achieved much better results than existing IQA methods, while the winning method can demonstrate state-of-the-art performance.

We choose PSNR, SSIM [54] and FSIM [59] as representative traditional IQA methods, PI [7] and NIQE [35] as representative blind IQA methods, and LPIPS [61], DISTS [12], PieAPP [39] and SWD [19] as representative deep-learning based methods.

11 of 13 participating teams achieve an SRCC score higher than 0.75 on PIPAL, which significantly surpasses the highest performance of existing algorithms (0.65). The champion team achieves an SRCC score of 0.799 and a PLCC score of 0.790, refreshing the state-of-the-art performance on PIPAL.
The top three teams in the challenge all achieve competitive results with existing methods on TID2013 and LIVE, showing their good generalization ability on traditional distortion types.

One can observe that the objective scores predicted by the top solutions have higher correlations with the subjective evaluations than existing methods. ... the correlation between the evaluation of the champion solution (1st) and the subjective score reaches 0.95.

It has been noticed that the contradiction between the quantitative evaluation results and the real perceptual quality is increasing [6, 7, 20]. This will also affect the development of image processing algorithms, if the IQA methods cannot objectively compare their perceptual quality.
Zhang et al. [61] propose to use pre-trained deep networks to calculate the perceptual similarity and achieve good results.

sub_claims.nuance: The challenge paper compares deep-feature-based IQA methods (LPIPS, DISTS, PieAPP, SWD, plus the 13 deep-learning team submissions) against traditional metrics (PSNR, SSIM, FSIM) and blind metrics (PI, NIQE) on natural-image datasets (PIPAL, TID2013, LIVE). The deep-learning / DFD-style methods substantially outperform the traditional baselines, supporting the manuscript's framing.

phrasings_tried: Does gu2022 show that LPIPS / deep-feature-distance (DFD) IQA methods outperform commonly used (traditional) IQ metrics for natural images?
Do the NTIRE perceptual-IQA challenge results in gu2022 demonstrate deep-learning-based IQA methods surpassing PSNR / SSIM / FSIM / NIQE / PI baselines on perceptual quality of natural images?
Is gu2022 evidence that deep-feature-based perceptual quality metrics correlate better with human subjective scores on natural images than classical IQA metrics?
Does gu2022 benchmark LPIPS-style deep IQA against traditional IQA metrics and find the deep methods superior on natural-image perceptual quality?

section_checklist.section: The NTIRE Challenge on Perceptual IQA
HIFIC Compression ESRGAN x2 SPSR (team description)

attestation.indirect_attribution_check: gu2022 is itself the primary report of the NTIRE 2021 perceptual IQA challenge results. It cites LPIPS (Zhang et al. [61]), DISTS [12], PieAPP [39], SWD [19], and the PIPAL dataset [20] as predecessors, but the head-to-head benchmark numbers comparing DFD-style methods against traditional metrics on PIPAL/TID2013/LIVE are reported in this paper as a primary result of the challenge.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript invokes gu2022 alongside refs 28–32 to support a general framing that DFDs outperform commonly used IQ metrics on natural images. gu2022's PIPAL test set focuses substantially on GAN-based perceptual distortions (which are arguably the harder and more relevant case), but the paper also reports results on TID2013 and LIVE (classical natural-image IQA datasets) and frames its contribution against 'existing IQA methods' broadly, so the framing usage is consistent with the source's own framing.

provenance_note: PROVENANCE CAVEAT: The fetched PDF at pdfs/gu2022/ is the NTIRE 2021 Perceptual Image Quality Assessment Challenge paper (Gu et al., arXiv:2105.03072, pub_date 2021-06-28), not the NTIRE 2022 challenge paper that the citekey 'gu2022' nominally refers to. Per the v1 ledger note, this substitution was carried over because the structurally similar 2021 challenge paper still supports the manuscript's framing about deep-feature-distance methods outperforming traditional IQ metrics on natural-image perceptual quality (the 2022 challenge is a direct continuation of the same benchmark series, on the same PIPAL dataset family, with the same comparison structure). Bib-level fix (substituting the actual 2022 paper, e.g. Gu et al. NTIRE 2022 report) should be tracked as a separate bibliography remediation; this evidence record verifies the claim against the substituted 2021 paper.
